ralplan_architect_review.summary: Architect approved the Rust-owned safety/data core, audited read-only connector registry, SQLite-backed local persistence, Command Center UI mapping, and Ultragoal handoff. Strongest antithesis was that frontend-first/plugin-heavy Tauri could ship faster, but the plan correctly prioritizes safety and privacy. Recommended improvements were applied before Critic review: source-coverage scoring contract, Windows packaging target/signing distinction, explicit MIT license ADR, concrete Ultragoal evidence files, and first vertical slice.

ralplan_critic_review.summary: Critic approved the revised PRD, test spec, and implementation plan as actionable for Ultragoal handoff. Critic verified clarity, verifiability, completeness, principle-option consistency, fair alternatives, risk mitigation, deliberate-mode pre-mortem and expanded tests, and durable Ultragoal completion tracking. No blocking issues found.

hard_invariants: Never consume, redeem, or mutate reset credits.
Never print, store, log, display, fixture, or commit auth tokens, account secrets, or full auth file contents.
All auth-adjacent reads must be read-only and guarded.
Undocumented read-only endpoints are allowed and enabled by default only behind audited connector code and safety checks.

ultragoal_handoff.definition_of_done: Do not mark complete until every PRD acceptance criterion, test-spec ledger item, documentation requirement, screenshot requirement, packaging smoke, and safety invariant has fresh evidence.
first_vertical_slice: One synthetic local-history fixture imported into SQLite.
One read-only reset-credit connector behind mock HTTP.
One dashboard summary command returning sanitized synthetic data.
One minimal Command Center dashboard screen rendering that data.
